title: Parameterize OpenBao RemoteKey per Keystone CR
description:
# GitHub Issue #279: Parameterize OpenBao RemoteKey per Keystone CR to eliminate cross-CR KV path collisions

**Category**: bug | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The Fernet and credential `PushSecret` builders hardcode the OpenBao `RemoteKey` to the same two literal paths for every Keystone CR in a cluster:

- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:423` — `RemoteKey: "openstack/keystone/fernet-keys"`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go:429` — `RemoteKey: "openstack/keystone/credential-keys"`

Every `PushSecret` CR — `{keystone.Name}-fernet-keys-backup` / `{keystone.Name}-credential-keys-backup` — is itself name-scoped, and the local `Secret` it selects is name-scoped too (`reconcile_fernet.go:417`, `reconcile_credential.go:423`). Only the remote KV-v2 path is flat-namespaced across CRs. Two Keystone CRs in the same Kubernetes namespace therefore share exactly two backing OpenBao objects — whichever CR's ESO push lands last wins, and either CR's deletion can race with the other CR's next push.

In production this is dormant (the conventional deployment runs a single Keystone per namespace), but it is load-bearing in the E2E suite, where `tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml:40` sets `parallel: 4` and every `tests/e2e/keystone/*/chainsaw-test.yaml` file lives in the same `openstack` namespace. Step 6 of `tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml:255-256` asserts that both KV-v2 paths are `not-found` after the `keystone-cleanup` CR is deleted — an assertion that cannot hold whenever a concurrent test's CR is mid-reconcile, because that CR's `PushSecret` is writing back to the shared path the cleanup test just purged.

The operator's own OpenBao policy (`deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl:14-20`) already rejects a wildcard over the Keystone subtree — for the orthogonal reason of protecting the read-only `openstack/keystone/db` credentials consumed by Keystone — so the flat two-path scheme is not even a policy simplification we are preserving.

**Motivation**: Reproduced on `feature/CC-0088` in CI run [24903037791/job 72927386373](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24903037791/job/72927386373) at `2026-04-24T17:53:33Z`. The `keystone-deletion-cleanup` step-6 retry loop polled for 30 s and observed both KV-v2 paths continuously present with `created_time=2026-04-24T17:53:11.602847833Z` (Fernet) — created 10 s after the cleanup test's `kubectl delete` at `17:53:01` and exactly matching the `keystone-brownfield-database` step-2 apply at `17:53:11`. The adjacent `keystone-credential-rotation` and `keystone-concurrent-cr-conflicts` CRs running in parallel contribute additional overlapping writers. Step-4's `PushSecret NotFound` assertion passes cleanly on the cleanup test's own backup objects — the leak is purely the shared remote path being re-created by a neighbour.

Previously observed on `main` in CI run [24839732116](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24839732116) at `14:31:30` (recorded in auto-memory `project_shared_openbao_kv_path.md`), 16 s after `keystone-cleanup` deletion, same failure mode. Both occurrences match the same timestamp-evidenced race; this is a reliably reproducible flake rather than a one-off. PR #271 (CC-0091) closed the independent adoption-race leak in the operator itself and added the bounded-retry loop step-6 depends on, but the loop cannot tolerate an external, legitimate writer regenerating the path — only this issue can.

Spin-off from analysing the red `feature/CC-0088` E2E run; CC-0088 itself is unrelated (Envoy Gateway / HTTPRoute assertions).

**Proposed approach**:

1. Switch both builders to a per-CR remote key layout, e.g. `openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/fernet-keys` and `openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/credential-keys`. The CR name is already in scope in both functions (`fernetKeysPushSecret(keystone *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone)` at `reconcile_fernet.go:403`, symmetric at `reconcile_credential.go:409`), so the change is a one-line `fmt.Sprintf` in each. A nested subpath keeps the convention of the sibling `openstack/keystone/db` path used by `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml:27` and preserves the existing wildcard reads granted by `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl:34` (`kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*`).
2. Broaden the `push-keystone-keys.hcl` policy from two literal paths to two per-name patterns while still excluding `/db`:
   - `kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/fernet-keys`
   - `kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/credential-keys`

   The `+` single-segment glob (OpenBao/Vault capability template syntax) keeps the policy tight enough that a compromised ESO still cannot reach `openstack/keystone/db`, matching the `push-keystone-keys.hcl:14-20` rationale. Verify the `eso-management` wildcard (`kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*`) still covers the new read paths so the redundant-`read` capability remains redundant rather than load-bearing.
3. Update the step-6 chainsaw script in `tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml:255-256` to target the CR-specific paths (`kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/fernet-keys` etc.), so the retry loop asserts exactly the path the cleanup CR owned and cannot be perturbed by parallel tests.
4. Update both builder unit tests (`reconcile_fernet_test.go:412`, `reconcile_credential_test.go:416`) to the new expected `RemoteKey` string.
5. Update reconciler docs: `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:763-764`, `:803`, `:1341`, `:1376`, `:1510`, `:1550`, and `architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md:279` to reference `{keystone.Name}` in the path.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `fernetKeysPushSecret` and `credentialKeysPushSecret` emit per-CR `RemoteKey` values that embed `keystone.Name`.
- [ ] `push-keystone-keys.hcl` grants `create/update/read/delete` on the per-name patterns only, still denies access to `openstack/keystone/db`, and does so under a policy comment that captures the rationale for including the name segment.
- [ ] Two Keystone CRs named `keystone-a` and `keystone-b` in the same namespace produce four distinct populated KV-v2 paths (`.../keystone-a/{fernet,credential}-keys`, `.../keystone-b/{fernet,credential}-keys`) — verifiable by hand with `bao kv list kv-v2/openstack/keystone/` or by extending `tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts` with a positive KV assertion.
- [ ] `chainsaw/keystone-deletion-cleanup` step-6 asserts the `keystone-cleanup`-scoped paths and passes while any of `keystone-autoscaling`, `keystone-credential-rotation`, `keystone-brownfield-database`, or `keystone-concurrent-cr-conflicts` run in parallel (`parallel: 4`).
- [ ] No regression in `chainsaw/keystone-fernet-rotation` or `chainsaw/keystone-credential-rotation` — PushSecret still reaches `Ready=True` on the new path.
- [ ] Deletion-cleanup flake does not recur across 10 consecutive CI runs (manual `gh workflow run e2e.yaml` trigger) to confirm the race is closed, matching the validation bar used for #271.
- [ ] Docs: the `{keystone.Name}` segment appears in every reference-doc location that previously listed the flat path, including the PushSecret table at `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:763-764` and the rotation sections at `:1341/:1376/:1510/:1550`.
- [ ] Migration note added to `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`: pre-existing clusters carrying data at `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet,credential}-keys` will see the operator write a new path on next reconcile; the old paths are orphaned but harmless (read-only from Keystone's perspective — the live Secret is driven from the local Kubernetes `Secret`, not the OpenBao backup) and can be hard-deleted manually. Call this out explicitly so upgraders don't interpret the stale path as a leak.

**Refs**: CC-0079 (openbao-finalizer), CC-0083 (PushSecret write policy), CC-0091 (adoption race), #271, #273. Auto-memory: `project_shared_openbao_kv_path.md`.

**Labels:** bug

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/279

stories.title: Operator engineer isolates OpenBao KV paths per Keystone CR
stories.want: the Fernet and credential backup PushSecrets of each Keystone CR to write to a CR-scoped OpenBao KV-v2 path
stories.so_that: two Keystone CRs in the same Kubernetes namespace never overwrite each other's backups and a deletion of one CR cannot race a sibling CR's next push
criteria: fernetKeysPushSecret returns RemoteKey equal to openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/fernet-keys
credentialKeysPushSecret returns RemoteKey equal to openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/credential-keys
The CR-Name segment appears literally in RemoteRef.RemoteKey, not only in PushSecret.metadata.Name
Two CRs named keystone-a and keystone-b produce four distinct kv-v2 paths (verifiable via `bao kv list kv-v2/openstack/keystone/` under acceptance)

stories.title: Security reviewer keeps least-privilege on the ESO write path
stories.want: the push-keystone-keys.hcl policy to grant create/update/read/delete only on the per-name Fernet and credential backup paths and still deny any access to openstack/keystone/db
stories.so_that: broadening the path scheme does not accidentally widen the blast radius of a compromised ESO token (CC-0083 rationale)
criteria: Policy uses OpenBao/Vault single-segment glob (`+`) for the CR-name position on both kv-v2/data/... and kv-v2/metadata/... paths
Policy continues to exclude openstack/keystone/db — no wildcard that would subsume /db is introduced
Capabilities on every listed path remain [create, update, read, delete] — no capability is added or removed
Policy comment captures the reason the name segment is now a `+` glob and why the exclusion of /db is still intact

stories.title: E2E test author runs deletion-cleanup under parallel:4 without flakes
stories.want: the deletion-cleanup chainsaw test step-6 bao-cli loop to assert absence of the keystone-cleanup-scoped KV paths, not the shared flat paths
stories.so_that: a concurrent keystone-autoscaling, keystone-credential-rotation, keystone-brownfield-database, or keystone-concurrent-cr-conflicts test cannot repopulate the asserted paths and mask the cleanup test's own behaviour
criteria: step-6 iterates kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/fernet-keys and kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/credential-keys
Error messages name the CR-scoped path that leaked, not the shared flat path
Existing bounded 30s / 3s retry loop and set -u semantics are preserved (no regression of CC-0091 loop)
Running the suite under parallel:4 no longer reproduces the 2026-04-24 and CI run 24839732116 flake on the step-6 assertion

stories.title: Concurrent CR test positively asserts per-CR KV isolation
stories.want: tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts to include a positive assertion that both keystone-concurrent-a and keystone-concurrent-b populate four distinct kv-v2 paths
stories.so_that: cross-CR path collision is guarded by CI, not only by manual `bao kv list` verification
criteria: A new step after both CRs reach Ready=True execs `bao kv get` against all four CR-scoped paths and asserts each returns exit 0
The step also asserts the legacy shared path openstack/keystone/fernet-keys is NOT created during the run (no writer still emits it)
The step inherits the openbao-0 / openbao-system exec pattern and root-token extraction from deletion-cleanup step-6
Failure output names the specific path that did not exist

stories.title: Operator reading the reconciler reference understands the per-CR layout
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to consistently reference `{keystone.Name}` in every mention of the Fernet and credential KV-v2 backup paths
stories.so_that: a reader building a multi-Keystone-per-namespace deployment sees no residual mention of the flat two-path scheme and the known-limitation call-out is removed
criteria: All of lines 763, 764, 780, 803, 1007, 1341, 1376, 1510, 1550 (pre-change numbering) now show `{keystone.Name}` in the path
The `> **Known limitation:**` block at lines 779-784 is deleted or rewritten to describe the per-CR layout as the normative model
A migration note is added describing orphaned flat paths on upgraded clusters and how to remove them
The DeletionPolicy=Delete code fragment at line 792-808 uses the parameterised RemoteKey example

stories.title: Cluster operator upgrading an existing deployment understands migration impact
stories.want: a migration note in the reconciler reference explaining that pre-existing clusters will orphan their flat-path backups on first reconcile after upgrade
stories.so_that: I do not interpret the stale kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet,credential}-keys paths as an operator leak and I know the safe manual cleanup procedure
criteria: Note explicitly states the old paths are orphaned but harmless (live Keystone state is driven from the local Kubernetes Secret, not the OpenBao backup)
Note lists the `bao kv metadata delete` command that removes the stale flat paths
Note is cross-linked from the PushSecret overview section so readers find it during an upgrade review
Note references feature id CC-0093 so it can be traced back to this change

stories.title: Reviewer verifies that the eso-management read wildcard still covers the new paths
stories.want: the planning and implementation steps to explicitly check that kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/* in deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl:34 still covers kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{name}/{fernet,credential}-keys
stories.so_that: the redundant-read capability on push-keystone-keys.hcl remains genuinely redundant (policy-portability only) and the management-cluster ESO role keeps read on the new path without a second grant
criteria: Implementation notes state that `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` is a multi-segment glob in OpenBao KV-v2 and covers `keystone/<name>/<leaf>` — with a citation
If the above is NOT true under OpenBao's actual glob semantics, the plan upgrades eso-management.hcl too (explicit task)
Verification is part of PR review — not assumed silently
A code-comment reference to this verification exists in push-keystone-keys.hcl

requirements.description: The fernetKeysPushSecret builder SHALL emit RemoteKey equal to `openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/fernet-keys` for any non-empty keystone.Name
requirements.rationale: Eliminates the cross-CR KV path collision documented in the issue; the local Secret is already name-scoped, only the remote path is currently flat

scenarios.name: Named CR yields nested path
scenarios.when: fernetKeysPushSecret is called with keystone.Name = "keystone-cleanup"
scenarios.then: the returned PushSecret has Spec.Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey == "openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/fernet-keys"
and_then: Spec.Selector.Secret.Name remains "{name}-fernet-keys"
ObjectMeta.Name remains "{name}-fernet-keys-backup"

scenarios.name: Distinct CR names yield distinct remote paths
scenarios.when: fernetKeysPushSecret is called twice with names "keystone-a" and "keystone-b"
scenarios.then: the two RemoteKey values differ and each contains the caller's name as a path segment
and_then: Neither value equals the legacy flat string "openstack/keystone/fernet-keys"

scenarios.name: DeletionPolicy and SecretStore invariants preserved
scenarios.when: fernetKeysPushSecret is called for any CR
scenarios.then: Spec.DeletionPolicy == PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete and SecretStoreRefs == [{Kind:ClusterSecretStore, Name:openbao-cluster-store}]
and_then: The CC-0079 openbao-finalizer wiring is not weakened

requirements.description: The credentialKeysPushSecret builder SHALL emit RemoteKey equal to `openstack/keystone/{keystone.Name}/credential-keys` for any non-empty keystone.Name
requirements.rationale: Symmetric fix to REQ-001 for the credential-keys backup path

scenarios.name: Named CR yields nested path
scenarios.when: credentialKeysPushSecret is called with keystone.Name = "keystone-cleanup"
scenarios.then: the returned PushSecret has Spec.Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey == "openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/credential-keys"
and_then: Spec.Selector.Secret.Name remains "{name}-credential-keys"

scenarios.name: Distinct CR names yield distinct remote paths
scenarios.when: credentialKeysPushSecret is called twice with names "keystone-a" and "keystone-b"
scenarios.then: the two RemoteKey values differ and each contains the caller's name as a path segment
and_then: Neither value equals the legacy flat string "openstack/keystone/credential-keys"

scenarios.name: DeletionPolicy and SecretStore invariants preserved
scenarios.when: credentialKeysPushSecret is called for any CR
scenarios.then: Spec.DeletionPolicy == PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete and SecretStoreRefs == [{Kind:ClusterSecretStore, Name:openbao-cluster-store}]
and_then: The CC-0079 openbao-finalizer wiring is not weakened

requirements.description: The push-keystone-keys.hcl OpenBao policy SHALL grant [create, update, read, delete] on both the data and metadata sub-paths for the CR-name-scoped fernet-keys and credential-keys leaves only
requirements.rationale: Keeps the policy wide enough for any {name}-scoped CR but tight enough to exclude openstack/keystone/db, preserving the CC-0083 invariant

scenarios.name: Per-name data paths granted
scenarios.when: policy file deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl is parsed
scenarios.then: it contains exactly four path blocks: kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/fernet-keys and kv-v2/{data,metadata}/openstack/keystone/+/credential-keys
and_then: Each block lists capabilities = ["create", "update", "read", "delete"]

scenarios.name: db path still excluded
scenarios.when: the policy is rendered against request paths kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/db and kv-v2/metadata/openstack/keystone/db
scenarios.then: no block matches — the policy grants no capabilities on those paths
and_then: The `+` single-segment glob does NOT match a literal path segment of `db` that has no subsequent /fernet-keys or /credential-keys suffix

scenarios.name: Policy comment documents rationale
scenarios.when: the file header comment is read
scenarios.then: it explicitly states why the `+` segment is used, why /db exclusion is preserved, and cross-references feature id CC-0093
and_then: The previous CC-0079 / CC-0083 comments remain — new rationale is added, not replaced

requirements.description: The deletion-cleanup chainsaw step-6 retry loop SHALL assert absence of kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/fernet-keys and kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/credential-keys, not the shared flat paths
requirements.rationale: The shared paths are no longer written by the operator after REQ-001/REQ-002; asserting them is both ineffective (other CRs cannot repopulate them) and inaccurate (they will not be there in the happy path either). The correct assertion targets the cleanup CR's own paths

scenarios.name: Script targets CR-scoped paths
scenarios.when: tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml step-6 script is read
scenarios.then: the for-loop lists exactly kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/fernet-keys and kv-v2/openstack/keystone/keystone-cleanup/credential-keys
and_then: The previously hard-coded strings at lines 255-256 are removed

scenarios.name: Retry loop semantics preserved
scenarios.when: step-6 runs against a cluster where keystone-cleanup is gone but other CRs are mid-reconcile
scenarios.then: the 30 s bounded retry with 3 s interval still governs the assertion, exit 0 on first all-clear sweep
and_then: The set -u, date +%s guard and per-path error-on-timeout lines from CC-0091 remain intact

scenarios.name: Parallel-tolerant
scenarios.when: step-6 runs concurrently with keystone-autoscaling, keystone-credential-rotation, keystone-brownfield-database, or keystone-concurrent-cr-conflicts
scenarios.then: the assertion cannot be re-populated by a neighbour — the keystone-cleanup/<leaf> paths are owned exclusively by keystone-cleanup
and_then: The bounded loop converges within the 30 s budget on a healthy run

requirements.description: The concurrent-cr-conflicts chainsaw test SHOULD include a positive assertion that all four per-CR kv-v2 paths exist after both CRs are Ready=True
requirements.rationale: Locks in per-CR KV isolation as a regression guard, complementing the negative assertion in deletion-cleanup

scenarios.name: Four paths present
scenarios.when: after step-2 asserts both concurrent CRs are Ready=True
scenarios.then: a new step execs bao kv get against each of kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{keystone-concurrent-a,keystone-concurrent-b}/{fernet-keys,credential-keys} and expects exit 0
and_then: A 60 s bounded retry budget with 3 s interval is used, matching step-6 of deletion-cleanup

scenarios.name: Legacy flat path absent
scenarios.when: the same step runs
scenarios.then: bao kv get on kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys and kv-v2/openstack/keystone/credential-keys returns non-zero (not-found)
and_then: This proves no path still collides between the two CRs

scenarios.name: Failure surface names the missing path
scenarios.when: a path is missing on timeout
scenarios.then: the script emits `ERROR: <path> missing after 60s` to stderr for each absent path and exits non-zero
and_then: The catch block dumps openbao pod state for triage

requirements.description: The unit tests TestReconcileFernetKeys_PushSecretRemoteKey and TestReconcileCredentialKeys_PushSecretRemoteKey SHALL assert the new nested RemoteKey layout
requirements.rationale: Pins REQ-001 / REQ-002 at the Go builder level, independent of E2E

scenarios.name: Fernet builder test updated
scenarios.when: TestReconcileFernetKeys_PushSecretExists (reconcile_fernet_test.go) runs under the CR name test-keystone
scenarios.then: the assertion on Spec.Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey is Equal("openstack/keystone/test-keystone/fernet-keys")
and_then: No other assertion in the same test changes — Secret name and ObjectMeta.Name still use the name-scoped value

scenarios.name: Credential builder test updated
scenarios.when: TestReconcileCredentialKeys_PushSecretExists (reconcile_credential_test.go) runs under the CR name test-keystone
scenarios.then: the assertion on Spec.Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey is Equal("openstack/keystone/test-keystone/credential-keys")
and_then: No other assertion in the same test changes

scenarios.name: Distinct-name isolation test added
scenarios.when: a new table-driven unit test iterates names [keystone-a, keystone-b]
scenarios.then: each call to fernetKeysPushSecret / credentialKeysPushSecret yields a distinct RemoteKey containing the iterated name
and_then: Locks in the one-to-one mapping between CR name and RemoteKey at the builder level

requirements.description: The reconciler reference documentation SHALL reference `{keystone.Name}` in every location that currently shows the flat KV-v2 path
requirements.rationale: Documentation drift silently hides the new invariant from readers building multi-CR deployments

scenarios.name: All enumerated locations updated
scenarios.when: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md is grep'd after the change for `openstack/keystone/(fernet|credential)-keys`
scenarios.then: every remaining hit includes `{keystone.Name}` as a path segment and the original flat form appears only inside the explicit migration-note block

scenarios.name: Known-limitation block removed or rewritten
scenarios.when: the `> **Known limitation:**` block at 779-784 is read
scenarios.then: it is either deleted or rewritten to describe the per-CR layout as the normative model — no stale limitation text survives
and_then: The change references CC-0093 in a migration-note context

scenarios.name: Migration note present
scenarios.when: the PushSecret overview section is read
scenarios.then: a migration-note subsection explains that pre-existing clusters will orphan kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet,credential}-keys on first reconcile, that the data is harmless, and gives the bao command to hard-delete it
and_then: Note references CC-0093 by feature id

requirements.description: The architecture submodule mention of the flat path (architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md:279) SHALL NOT be modified from this worktree
requirements.rationale: architecture/ is a git submodule pointing at github.com/C5C3/C5C3 — per repo rule NO_SUBMODULE_MODIFICATIONS, writes from this worktree are forbidden and would create divergent git state across repos. The correction must be delivered as a separate upstream PR in the submodule repository

scenarios.name: Submodule left untouched
scenarios.when: git diff --submodule HEAD after the implementation PR is committed
scenarios.then: no subproject commit change is reported for architecture/
and_then: The feature PR body documents the upstream follow-up as a deferred action

scenarios.name: Deferred follow-up recorded
scenarios.when: the deferred_items section of this plan is reviewed
scenarios.then: an entry names the upstream file, the required change, and the rationale that it cannot be applied from this worktree

scenarios.name: Parent-repo docs are fully self-consistent without the submodule fix
scenarios.when: a reader follows only docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md and deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl
scenarios.then: they get a complete and correct picture of the per-CR layout without needing the submodule update

requirements.description: The push-keystone-keys.hcl policy SHOULD NOT widen read/write access to kv-v2/openstack/keystone/db under any CR name
requirements.rationale: Negative-test invariant for REQ-003; the `+` glob is specifically chosen to avoid this case, but it must be asserted

scenarios.name: db path under name segment not matched
scenarios.when: a test renders the policy against path kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/db
scenarios.then: no block matches — OpenBao denies the request
and_then: Because `+` requires exactly one segment AND the literal suffix /fernet-keys or /credential-keys

scenarios.name: db path at deeper depth not matched
scenarios.when: a test renders the policy against path kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/db/fernet-keys (hypothetical)
scenarios.then: it matches the fernet-keys pattern with `+`=db and capabilities apply
and_then: This is acceptable: `db` is not a CR name in our deployment, and the path does not conflict with the existing read-only /db literal

scenarios.name: Comment documents the edge case
scenarios.when: the policy comment is read
scenarios.then: it explicitly notes that CR names cannot be literally `db` and references feature id CC-0093

requirements.description: The implementation SHALL NOT introduce a new operator config field, new API version, or new CRD version
requirements.rationale: The CR name is already in scope in both builder functions; the fix is a pure RemoteKey change and must not pull in unrelated API surface

scenarios.name: No new CRD field
scenarios.when: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1 is diffed after the change
scenarios.then: no new Spec field, enum, or validation rule is added

scenarios.name: No new runtime flag
scenarios.when: operators/keystone/cmd and operators/keystone/internal are diffed
scenarios.then: no new command-line flag, environment variable, or config knob is introduced

scenarios.name: No breaking-signature change
scenarios.when: fernetKeysPushSecret and credentialKeysPushSecret signatures are inspected
scenarios.then: they continue to accept a single *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone argument — no helper struct or options pattern is added

tasks.title: Parameterize RemoteKey in fernetKeysPushSecret (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:423) using fmt.Sprintf("openstack/keystone/%s/fernet-keys", keystone.Name); update the godoc at :396-:402 so the kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys mention becomes kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{name}/fernet-keys and references feature id CC-0093 (REQ-001, REQ-010)

Parameterize RemoteKey in credentialKeysPushSecret (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go:429) using fmt.Sprintf("openstack/keystone/%s/credential-keys", keystone.Name); update the godoc at :402-:408 symmetrically and reference CC-0093 (REQ-002, REQ-010)

Update reconcile_fernet_test.go:412 to Equal("openstack/keystone/test-keystone/fernet-keys") and add TestFernetKeysPushSecret_RemoteKeyIsCRScoped (table-driven over names [keystone-a, keystone-b, keystone-cleanup]) and TestFernetKeysPushSecret_PreservesDeletionPolicyAndStoreRef in the same file (REQ-001, REQ-006)

The note states: (a) on upgrade to CC-0093 the operator will write to the new `{name}`-scoped paths on next reconcile, (b) the old flat paths `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet,credential}-keys` are orphaned but harmless (live Keystone state is driven from the local Kubernetes Secret, not the OpenBao backup), (c) the recommended cleanup commands are `bao kv metadata delete kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys` and `bao kv metadata delete kv-v2/openstack/keystone/credential-keys`. Do NOT modify the submodule content from this worktree (REQ-008)

implementation_notes:
## Architecture summary

The fix is a two-site string-format change in the operator (reconcile_fernet.go:423, reconcile_credential.go:429), a one-file policy rewrite (push-keystone-keys.hcl), a two-file E2E update (deletion-cleanup + concurrent-cr-conflicts), and a documentation-consistency sweep (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md at nine pre-existing locations plus a new migration-note subsection). The CR name is already in scope in both builders — no new plumbing, no new CRD field, no new flag (REQ-010).

## Data flow (unchanged except the highlighted segment)

```
Keystone CR (name=N)
  └─ reconcile_fernet.go / reconcile_credential.go
       └─ fernetKeysPushSecret(ks) / credentialKeysPushSecret(ks)
            └─ builds PushSecret { ObjectMeta.Name = "<N>-{fernet,credential}-keys-backup",
                                    Spec.Selector.Secret.Name = "<N>-{fernet,credential}-keys",
     <<< CHANGE >>>          Spec.Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey = "openstack/keystone/<N>/{fernet,credential}-keys"
                                    Spec.DeletionPolicy = Delete }  # CC-0079 wiring intact
```

OpenBao authorization of the new path is governed by push-keystone-keys.hcl bound (alongside eso-management.hcl) to the management-cluster ESO Kubernetes auth role. The eso-management.hcl wildcard `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*` is the read-side grant and (per upstream KV-v2 glob semantics) covers any nested leaf — a tight reading of the `*` operator in ACL path rules. Task 2.2 makes that semantics verification load-bearing, not assumed.

## Architecture patterns referenced

- Builder-returns-*PushSecret: identical shape in reconcile_fernet.go:403 and reconcile_credential.go:409 (symmetric pair pattern used repeatedly in the sub-reconcilers — see also openBaoBackupPushSecretNames at reconcile_secrets.go:156).
- Per-CR name-scoping: already in use for PushSecret.metadata.Name and Spec.Selector.Secret.Name — the RemoteKey is the last hold-out and REQ-001/REQ-002 close the gap.
- Chainsaw bounded-retry shell pattern from CC-0091: reused verbatim in step-6 of deletion-cleanup and in the new concurrent-cr-conflicts step (see tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml:250-284).
- OpenBao policy header-comment convention: push-keystone-keys.hcl:5-43 — every rationale paragraph is cross-referenced with its feature id (CC-0079, CC-0083). CC-0093 must be appended in the same style.

## Risks and how to avoid them

1. **OpenBao glob semantics regression.** If the deployed OpenBao version does NOT honour `+` as a single-segment glob in ACL policies, PushSecret writes will 403 at runtime and PushSecret.Status never reaches Ready. Task 2.2 specifically blocks on documenting the upstream reference before merging. Fallback: enumerate known CR names (production runs a single `keystone` CR, but we intentionally support arbitrary names — this would regress REQ-001 acceptance).
2. **Upgrade orphan confusion.** A reader sees stale data at `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet,credential}-keys` and opens a bug against the operator for leaking. The migration-note subsection (Task 4.7) explicitly labels these as harmless and gives the cleanup command — this was also called out as an acceptance criterion in the source issue.
3. **Submodule divergence.** architecture/ is a git submodule; any write from this worktree crosses independent git contexts. Per repo rule 12 and REQ-008, the plan strictly forbids modifying the submodule and delivers the required update as a deferred upstream PR.
4. **Test symmetry drift.** The two builders have symmetric fates — the table-driven distinct-name tests added by 1.3 and 1.4 lock that in, preventing a future refactor from silently re-unifying the paths.
5. **Unit-only coverage gap.** A pure builder-level test would pass even if the Ensure/Patch layer regressed back to a literal string. Integration-level tests at 3.3 guard against that by exercising the full reconcile with envtest.

## Key files

- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go (builder — CHANGE)
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go (builder — CHANGE)
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet_test.go (unit tests — CHANGE)
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential_test.go (unit tests — CHANGE)
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go (envtest — ADD)
- deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl (policy — CHANGE)
- deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl (READ-ONLY reference — verify wildcard covers the new read path)
- deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-db.yaml (READ-ONLY reference — path-convention sibling)
- tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml (step-6 — CHANGE)
- tests/e2e/keystone/concurrent-cr-conflicts/chainsaw-test.yaml (new positive-assertion step — CHANGE)
- docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (nine pre-existing locations + new migration note — CHANGE)
- architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md (SUBMODULE, READ-ONLY — follow-up PR)

## Deferred items

- **Upstream architecture-submodule doc fix.** architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md:279 still references `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys` as the backup path. Per repo rule 12 and REQ-008 this worktree cannot modify submodule content. Rationale: crossing git contexts from a worktree produces divergent submodule state. Timing: open the follow-up PR against github.com/C5C3/C5C3 immediately after CC-0093 merges, so the parent-repo documentation does not carry a dangling submodule reference.
- **Automated OpenBao policy test-harness.** The plan verifies policy HCL changes by reviewer checklist because no in-repo test framework executes policies against synthetic request paths today. Timing: consider adding a `go test` harness that instantiates an OpenBao dev server and exercises the policy against `keystone/+/{fernet,credential}-keys` plus `keystone/db` as a cross-cutting CC-0083 hardening follow-up.
- **Hard-delete of orphaned flat-path data on upgrade.** The migration note documents the manual `bao kv metadata delete` commands. A first-reconcile-time automation that performs this purge is explicitly out of scope — it would require the operator to hold a write grant on the legacy path, and granting that for a one-time migration is worse than leaving the paths orphaned (REQ-009 invariant).
- **Multi-Keystone-per-namespace E2E extension.** Concurrent-cr-conflicts already creates two CRs in the same namespace; after CC-0093 lands, we could extend it to run a Fernet rotation on both CRs in parallel and assert both PushSecrets reach Ready=True on their distinct paths. Timing: optional follow-up — REQ-005's positive KV-path assertion is the minimum necessary guard.
